name: PostGIS
pricing_notes: Fully open source (GPLv2); PostgreSQL extension; no licensing costs

auth.notes: PostgreSQL authentication; no separate PostGIS auth

performance.notes: Spatial queries with indexes sub-5ms; complex polygon intersections 10-100ms depending on complexity and data size

agent_readiness.notes: PostgreSQL extension adding spatial types, functions, and indexes. Geometry and Geography types. Spatial indexes (GiST, BRIN). ST_Contains, ST_Intersects, ST_Distance, ST_Buffer, ST_Union, raster operations. Python access via psycopg2/asyncpg/SQLAlchemy with GeoAlchemy2. Gold standard for production geospatial databases. Works with QGIS, GDAL, Mapbox, etc.

gotchas: Requires PostgreSQL — not a standalone service
SRID (coordinate reference system) mismatches cause silent errors or wrong results
Geography type for globe-accurate distances; Geometry for planar math — easy to mix up
Large geometry datasets need VACUUM ANALYZE and proper GiST indexes
best_when: Production geospatial databases requiring ACID transactions and spatial queries
Point-in-polygon, proximity search, routing catchment areas at scale
Combining spatial queries with regular relational data (JOIN spatial + business tables)
Applications already using PostgreSQL wanting to add geospatial capabilities
avoid_when: Pure analytics/OLAP workloads (BigQuery + spatial extensions may be better)
Real-time routing (use OSRM or Valhalla instead)
When H3 hexagonal indexing suffices for aggregation (simpler)
summary: PostGIS is the gold-standard geospatial extension for PostgreSQL, adding spatial types (geometry, geography), functions (ST_Contains, ST_Distance, ST_Buffer), and spatial indexes. Used in production by mapping, logistics, and location-based applications worldwide. Works with GeoAlchemy2 for Python ORM access. Best for combining spatial queries with relational data in ACID-compliant databases.
